title: athroisma haareri
intro: Athroisma haareri, also known as Australian swamp marigold or yellow button, is a flowering plant native to Australia and New Zealand. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Athroisma haareri include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Athroisma haareri can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its therapeutic properties.
